titleStmt.title: SII 29.49: original edition by P. N. Narasimha Murthy
No. 49. (A.R. No. 68 of 1961-62.) KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a pillar kept in the compound of Sōmēśvara temple (Museum No. 86-87.)

It records the gift of the village Uddaṁḍige in Ānemārga-70, free from all taxes, for the repairs to the temple of god Sōmēśvara and for feeding the ascetics therein by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Toṇvaya Chōḍa mahārāja of Kandūru, who was administering Kollipāke-7000 division. The king Tribhuvanamalladēva is stated to have been ruling over the empire from the capital Kalyāṇa. The record, engraved on different pillars is now broken into pieces.
It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, uttarāyaṇa sankrānti. It corresponds to 1087 A.D., December 25, [Saturday]. The Śaka year was 1009.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti samasta bhuvanā
śraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha
mahārājādhirāja para
mēsvara parama bhaṭṭārakaṁ
satyāśraya kuḷatiḷa
kaṁ caḷukyābharaṇaṁ
śrī matribhuvanamalladēva
ra vijaya rājyamutta
rōttarābhivriddhi prava
rddhamānamācaṁdrārka tā

raṁbaraṁ kalyāṇada nele
vīḍinoḷsukha saṁkathāvinō
dadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttumire
tatpāda padmōpajīvi
samadhigata paṁca mahā
śabda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ
vīra māhēśvaraṁ kōḍū
rppuravarēśvaraṁ sūryya
vaṁsōdbhava kuḷatiḷakaṁ
kāsyapa gōtra karikā
lānvaya nāmādi sama
sta praśasti sahitaṁ

śrī manmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ
kandūra toṇvaya cōḍama
hārāja koḷḷipāka Ērccā
siramumaṁ sukhadināḷu
ttamiṟdu śrī maccāḷukya
vikramagālada hanneraḍane
ya prabhava saṁvatsara dutta
rāyaṇa saṁkrānti puṁṇya

koḷḷi
pākeya śrīsōmēśvaradē
vargge biṭṭa khaṇḍasphuṭita jīrṇnō
ddhārakkaṁ tapōdhana(rggā)hā
radānakkaṁ mānemārggame
rppattaṟa baḷiya duddaṇḍegē
yaṁ sarvvamānyamāge
koṭṭar . Ī dharmaṁ naḍaya(yi)
sadiṟdarappodi gaṁge bāṇa
rāsi śrī parvvatado
ḷu pannīrcchāsira tapōdhanaraṁ
brāhmaṇaraṁ kavilegaḷaṁ
naḷida pāpa . śrī śrī śrī
Digital edition of SII 29.49 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
